reason: This is a standard STIG password policy hardening change for PAM. It involves a simple configuration edit to /etc/security/pwquality.conf to require uppercase characters in passwords. The fix is unlikely to cause system breakage or lockout as it only affects new password changes, not existing passwords. Follows recommended STIG patterns for authn/authz hardening.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential user lockouts if existing passwords are not updated to meet new requirements; Requires validation after change to ensure passwords comply
